Earnings/Fitness & Health/Nigeria
Creator earnings · Nigeria

How much do fitness & health YouTubers make in Nigeria?

Fitness & Health channels with an audience in Nigeria earn an RPM of roughly $0.8 to $1.7 per 1,000 views. That is Nigeria specifically, Africa’s biggest creator market, where a large audience and low local ad spend mean earnings come from volume rather than rate. RPM is what the creator keeps after YouTube's 45% cut and the views that never saw an ad, so it is the only earnings figure that reflects real take-home pay.

At that rate, one million monthly views from Nigerian viewers works out to roughly $840 to $1,680 (about ₦1,302,000 to ₦2,604,000) per month from ads alone, before any sponsorship or affiliate income. That is about 14% of what the same channel would earn from a US audience, because the local ad market pays less per view. Use the calculator below to estimate your own channel.

RPM · Nigerian audience
$0.8 – $1.7
per 1,000 views, what you keep
CPM (advertiser gross)
$1.5 – $3.1

from ads alone, up to $1,680

RPM is what hits your AdSense after YouTube's 45% cut and unmonetized views. CPM (advertiser gross) runs roughly $2–$3. These figures are YTGrowth estimates, not YouTube data.

The economics

What fitness & health creators earn in Nigeria.

01

Audience country sets the ad rate. A fitness & health view from a Nigerian viewer is worth roughly 14% of the same view from a US viewer, because Africa’s biggest creator market, where a large audience and low local ad spend mean earnings come from volume rather than rate.

02

Season swings the number. Q4 advertiser budgets push fitness & health RPM 30 to 40% above the Q1 floor, so a Nigerian channel earns noticeably more per view in November than in January.

03

The real money sits past AdSense. Established Nigerian fitness & health creators lean on coaching, training plans, and supplement or gear affiliates, which is priced on audience and niche rather than on the local ad rate.

By country

Fitness & Health RPM around the world.

CountryRPM per 1,000Per 1M views
United States$6.0 – $12$6,000 – $12,000
Australia$5.7 – $11$5,700 – $11,400
United Kingdom$5.4 – $11$5,400 – $10,800
Canada$5.3 – $11$5,280 – $10,560
India$1.2 – $2.4$1,200 – $2,400
Pakistan$1.0 – $1.9$960 – $1,920
Germany$4.3 – $8.6$4,320 – $8,640
Ukraine$1.3 – $2.6$1,320 – $2,640
Philippines$1.1 – $2.2$1,080 – $2,160
Indonesia$1.0 – $1.9$960 – $1,920
Nigeria$0.8 – $1.7$840 – $1,680

Same fitness & health content, different audience country. The RPM gap is driven by local advertiser spend, not by the channel.

Frequently asked

Fitness & Health in Nigeria, answered.

What Nigerian creators ask before they commit to this niche. Still curious? Get in touch.

How much do fitness & health YouTubers make in Nigeria?+

A fitness & health channel with a Nigerian audience typically earns an RPM of $0.8 to $1.7 per 1,000 views, or roughly ₦1,300 to ₦2,600 in local terms, which is what you keep after YouTube's 45% cut and un-monetised views. One million monthly views works out to roughly $840 to $1,680 (about ₦1,302,000 to ₦2,604,000) a month from ads alone.

How does Google pay fitness & health creators in Nigeria, and in what currency?+

AdSense pays in US dollars and your Nigerian bank converts to naira, so the ₦ total swings with the USD/NGN rate. Payout clears after the $100 threshold. The RPM figures on this page are the US-dollar amounts AdSense reports, which your bank converts to NGN, so the local total moves a little with the exchange rate.

Do fitness & health YouTubers in Nigeria pay tax on their earnings?+

YouTube income is taxable in Nigeria as self-employment income, and creators earning at scale typically register with the FIRS and file annually. This is general information, not tax advice, so check your own situation with a local accountant once the channel earns real money.

How much does a fitness & health YouTuber make per million views in Nigeria?+

At a Nigerian RPM of $0.8 to $1.7, one million views earns roughly $840 to $1,680 (about ₦1,302,000 to ₦2,604,000) from ads. Sponsorships and affiliates usually add more on top, and in a lower-RPM market like this one those extra streams often matter more than the ad revenue.

Which fitness & health videos pay the most in Nigeria?+

Within fitness & health, programs, nutrition, and equipment reviews command the highest ad rates because advertisers in those categories bid the most, and that holds in Nigeria just as it does elsewhere. Pair that with mid-roll ads on 8-minute-plus videos to lift RPM further.

How can a Nigerian fitness & health channel earn beyond the local ad rate?+

Most serious fitness & health creators in Nigeria earn more from coaching, training plans, and supplement or gear affiliates than from AdSense. This matters even more in a lower-RPM market, where the ad rate alone is thin, and a brand deal is priced on audience and niche, not on the local ad rate.

How can a Nigerian fitness & health channel earn US-level rates?+

Make globally-framed fitness & health content in English so a real share of views come from US, UK, Canada and Australia viewers. A Nigerian channel that earns half its views from tier-1 countries can multiply its RPM several times over without changing topic.

How many views does a Nigerian fitness & health channel need to make $5,000 (about ₦7,750,000) a month?+

At the middle of the Nigerian fitness & health RPM band, roughly 3,968,000 monthly views gets you to $5,000 (about ₦7,750,000) a month from ads. Affiliate links and sponsorships can reach it at fewer views, which is often the faster route in a lower-RPM market.
